title: Similarity Heatmap
learning_goal: Show that QK^T is a table of pairwise relevance scores, and that scaling prevents scores from becoming too extreme before softmax.

animation_sequence.action: Introduce Q and K cards with stable colors; show formula QK^T above.
Draw comparison lines from one query to all keys; populate one heatmap row.
animation_sequence.manim_hint: Create arrows/lines, then animate cells from low to high color intensity.

animation_sequence.action: Complete the heatmap for all query-key pairs.
animation_sequence.manim_hint: LaggedStart cell fills row by row.

animation_sequence.action: Show an over-sharp score pattern and label why this can make softmax too peaky.
animation_sequence.manim_hint: Pulse high-score cells, add small caption.

animation_sequence.action: Transform QK^T into QK^T / sqrt(d_k); soften heatmap intensity.
animation_sequence.manim_hint: TransformMatchingTex(formula_qkt, scale_term); animate cell colors toward calmer palette.

pixverse_support.usage: 4-second abstract transition before returning to exact Manim heatmap.
must_preserve: No replacement for Manim heatmap explanation

music_sfx_cues.purpose: Mark the moment when individual comparisons become a full relevance table.
Support the score-scaling visual without distracting from the formula.

acceptance_checks: Q and K colors match the production manifest.
Viewer can distinguish raw QK^T scores from scaled scores.
Scaling explanation uses exactly one just-in-time aside.
PixVerse transition does not contain readable math.
